ops.title: macOS CI hang is a pre-existing runner flake, not a drain bug
ops.body:
Commit 3d55ae2e (drain D2/D4/D5) was suspected of introducing a `-race` deadlock after macOS CI run 29624781344 was cancelled at the 10-minute cap. CI-log triage exonerated it and identified a recurring macOS-only flake.

**Evidence (4 cancelled macOS runs in ~7h, unrelated commits):**
- Run 29624781344: every package printed ok within 7s — `drain` passed in 1.184s — except `wire`, `wire/lifeproto`, `wire/wiretest`, which never printed; 9m47s of silence, then job cancel. The commit touched only `drain/`.
- Run 29597502038: `drain` and `trust` never printed.
- Run 29598211774: only `drain` never printed.
- Run 29603093633: **all 13 packages printed ok and the job still hung to the timeout** — the hang occurs after every test asserts success.
- `ubuntu-latest` runs the identical `./scripts/test.sh -race ./...` green in ~19s. The "stuck" package varies per run.

**Leading theory:** a spawned child inherits the test binary's stdout/stderr pipes and outlives it, so `go test`'s driver never sees EOF even after PASS (the all-ok-yet-hung run is the smoking gun). Alternative: per-UID process starvation (harness logs `RLIMIT_NPROC soft cap=710` on the runner).

**Why no goroutine dump exists:** `scripts/test.sh` passes `-timeout 600s` while the job cap is also 600s; the job-level kill wins by 6–19s every time, eating go test's SIGQUIT dump. Fix in flight: default the harness `-timeout` to 480s.

A diagnosis/fix lane (gpt-5.6-sol) is auditing every subprocess-spawning test (proc, daemon, drain, wire, wiretest, service) for pipe-holding or orphaned children. Until fixed, treat a macOS job cancelled at 10m with no FAIL/panic/DATA RACE in the log as this flake — do not revert the triggering commit.
